note: Primary and authoritative data sources for UK Migration Explorer. Each public-facing figure carries its own source, publication date, retrieval date and confidence level in the theme files.

sources.name: Home Office: Immigration system statistics
sources.covers: Asylum applications, decisions, backlog and support; visas granted by route (work, study, family); settlement and citizenship; safe and legal routes; small boats and irregular migration.
sources.updateFrequency: Quarterly (latest: year ending March 2026 for asylum, returns, visas, settlement and citizenship)

sources.name: ONS: Long-term international migration
sources.covers: Net migration, immigration and emigration estimates, and reason-for-migration breakdowns. The headline net migration measure.
sources.updateFrequency: Twice yearly (provisional; official statistics in development)

sources.name: ONS: Population estimates
sources.covers: UK mid-year population, used as a denominator for shares and per-person context.

sources.name: Census 2021/22
sources.publisher: ONS (England, Wales, Northern Ireland) and National Records of Scotland
sources.covers: Foreign-born population, country of birth, nationality, and regional distribution. The most reliable foreign-born stock figures, as the ONS survey-based series was discontinued in October 2022.
sources.updateFrequency: Every 10 years (last: 2021 for E&W/NI, 2022 for Scotland)

sources.name: The Migration Observatory (University of Oxford)
sources.covers: Neutral analysis and briefings on the migrant population, labour market, fiscal impact, and long-run trends, drawing on ONS, Census and Home Office data.

sources.name: National Audit Office
sources.covers: Cost and value-for-money of the asylum system and asylum accommodation contracts.
sources.updateFrequency: Occasional (latest: An analysis of the asylum system, December 2025)

sources.name: Office for Budget Responsibility
sources.covers: Fiscal impact of migration in forecasts and the Fiscal Risks and Sustainability report (estimates and scenarios, clearly labelled).
sources.updateFrequency: At fiscal events and in periodic reports

sources.name: Migration Advisory Committee
sources.covers: Salary thresholds, shortage occupations, and the economic and fiscal evidence on work migration routes.

sources.name: Tribunals statistics quarterly (HMCTS / Ministry of Justice)
sources.covers: Asylum and immigration appeals: lodged, decided, outstanding, success rates, and waiting times.

sources.name: House of Commons Library research briefings
sources.covers: Plain-English summaries of asylum statistics, small boat crossings, NHS overseas staff, and immigration fees, drawing on the primary sources above.

sources.name: Skills for Care: state of the adult social care workforce
sources.covers: Nationality composition of the adult social care workforce in England.

sources.name: Office for Statistics Regulation: guidance on migration and asylum statistics
sources.covers: Guidance on responsible interpretation of migration and asylum statistics: which comparisons are valid, how to handle revisions, and where published figures are commonly misread. Used for methodology and caveat design rather than as a source of figures.
